raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I was unexpectedly billed by XXXX for {$1500.00} related to the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Invoice # XXXX, issued XX/XX/year> ). I am a XXXX XXXXXXXX and believed I was using a low-cost, subscription-style program suitable for personal learning projects. 

Timeline : I last accessed XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year> for initial setup/testing. 

I did not log in, make API calls, or otherwise use the XXXX XXXX XXXX service during the disputed billing period ( July 2131, 2025 ). 

Despite this, XXXX invoiced me for enterprise-level usage I did not knowingly generate. 

My actions : On XX/XX/year>, I disputed the charge directly with XXXX Collections and requested cancellation and a refund. 

XXXX staff acknowledged my request and confirmed termination of the subscription effective XX/XX/year>. 

Despite repeated follow-ups, XXXX refused to reverse the charge, citing usage reports even though I provided screenshots showing no activity from my side. 

On XX/XX/year>, XXXX confirmed termination but still denied the refund, stating charges were genuine despite my lack of activity. 

Additional charges : On XX/XX/year>, I was billed again for {$2400.00} covering XX/XX/year> service, even though my cancellation was confirmed effective XX/XX/XXXX. 

This left me with nearly {$4000.00} in charges for services I did not knowingly use. XXXX refused to escalate or resolve the matter, instead redirecting me to technical teams while Collections continued to demand payment. 

I am a XXXX, not a business. I believed I was using an affordable subscription service and never expected enterprise-scale charges. I can not afford nearly {$4000.00} in unexpected bills this amount is equivalent to a XXXX XXXX XXXX. I am XXXX this XXXX, and I am concerned about the impact these disputed charges could have on my credit standing if unresolved.
